description_long: This house features a symmetrical, two-story design with a steeply pitched gable roof and two prominent chimneys. The exterior walls are finished in a light-colored plaster with exposed dark timber framing, characteristic of Tudor-style architecture. Windows are arranged in neat, symmetrical rows with wooden shutters and mullions, and the glass panes are divided into smaller sections. The main entryway is centrally located, featuring a wooden door with a small overhang supported by decorative brackets and flanked by pilasters. The roof is covered in weathered shingles, and the house sits on a raised stone or brick platform. The overall appearance is historic and stately, evoking a sense of old European residential charm.
description: A symmetrical, two-story Tudor-style house with exposed timber framing, a steep gable roof, and a central wooden entryway. The exterior features neat rows of shuttered windows and sits on a raised stone platform.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway with a wooden door, symmetrical windows with shutters, and the gable roof with two chimneys.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the house, the steep roof pitch, and the symmetrical window arrangement on the side wall.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with a similar arrangement of windows and timber framing but lacking a visible entryway.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the symmetrical window placement and the full height of the house.
attribute_entryway_design: Central wooden door with a small overhang, decorative brackets, and flanking pilasters on a raised platform.
